Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive report: ## Frontend Codebase Exploration Report I've thoroughly explored the Changelogue frontend codebase. Here's what I found: ### 1. **Layout Architecture** **Root Layout** (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/layout.tsx`): - Single root layout file for the entire application - Uses a **flex layout with sidebar + main content area**: - `` on the left (200px wide, dark background) - Right side contains: `
` (12px height bar) + `
` (scrollable) - All pages are wrapped by this layout - **No nested layouts** exist - only this one root layout **Key files:** - `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx` - Navigation component - `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/layout/header.tsx` - Breadcrumb header component --- ### 2. **Header/Navigation Components** **Global Header** (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/layout/header.tsx`): - Renders a **12px-high breadcrumb header** that displays the current page location - Dynamically generates breadcrumbs from URL pathname - Maps route segments to human-readable labels (e.g., "projects" → "Projects") - Filters out UUIDs from the breadcrumb display - Single unified header for all pages **Global Sidebar Navigation** (`/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx`): - Dark themed (dark gray background) - Fixed navigation items: Dashboard, Projects, Releases, Channels, Subscriptions - Shows active indicator on current section --- ### 3. **Page Structure - Top-Level Pages** All top-level pages (`/web/app/*/page.tsx`) include **their own page-level headers**: | Page | Location | Header Type | |------|----------|------------| | **Dashboard** | `/web/app/page.tsx` | No explicit header (just content with StatsCards) | | **Projects** | `/web/app/projects/page.tsx` | ✅ Has `

Projects

` + description + "New Project" button (lines 147-168) | | **Releases** | `/web/app/releases/page.tsx` | ✅ Has `

Releases

` + project filter dropdown (lines 135-173) | | **Channels** | `/web/app/channels/page.tsx` | ✅ Has `

Channels

` + "New Channel" button (lines 54-79) | | **Subscriptions** | `/web/app/subscriptions/page.tsx` | ✅ Has `

Subscriptions

` + "New Subscription" button (lines 56-81) | | **Sources** | `/web/app/sources/page.tsx` | ✅ Has `

All Sources

` + description (lines 35-39) | --- ### 4. **Header Duplication - Critical Finding** **There IS header duplication:** - **Global header** (from root layout): Renders breadcrumb at top of page (e.g., "Projects", "Releases") - **Page-level headers**: Each page renders its own `

` title section with additional UI (buttons, filters) **Example from Projects page:** - Global header shows: `"Projects"` (breadcrumb) - Page-level section shows: `"Projects"` (h1) + description + "New Project" button This means users see the page title **twice** - once in the thin breadcrumb bar and once as the main page heading. --- ### 5. **Detail Page Structure** Dynamic pages render their content through client components: | Route | Component | Structure | |-------|-----------|-----------| | `/projects/[id]` | `ProjectDetail` | Has internal header zone with title, description, action buttons, and tab navigation (lines 183-287) | | `/releases/[id]` | `ReleaseDetail` | Has back link, title, metadata display, and cards (lines 122-202) | | `/projects/[id]/semantic-releases/[srId]` | `SemanticReleaseDetail` | (Rendered as client component) | | `/projects/[id]/edit` | `ProjectEdit` | (Rendered as client component) | **Key observation:** Detail pages have their own internal headers/titles, NOT relying on the global breadcrumb header. --- ### 6. **Component Organization** ``` /web/components/ ├── layout/ │ ├── header.tsx (global breadcrumb) │ └── sidebar.tsx (global nav) ├── dashboard/ │ ├── stats-cards.tsx │ ├── recent-releases.tsx │ └── activity-feed.tsx ├── projects/ │ ├── project-detail.tsx (has internal header + tabs) │ ├── project-edit.tsx │ └── project-form.tsx ├── releases/ │ └── release-detail.tsx (has internal header) ├── semantic-releases/ │ └── semantic-release-detail.tsx └── ui/ ├── provider-badge.tsx ├── version-chip.tsx ├── section-label.tsx └── [...other UI components] ``` --- ### 7. **Summary of Header Issues** **Duplication Points:** 1. **Main list pages** (Projects, Releases, Channels, Subscriptions): - Global breadcrumb header: Shows "Projects", "Releases", etc. - Page-level h1: Duplicates the title again with styling - **Result:** Title appears twice on the page 2. **Detail pages** (ProjectDetail, ReleaseDetail): - Global breadcrumb: Tries to show path like "Projects / [uuid]" (UUID filtered out, just shows "Projects") - Page-level detail header: Shows actual title inside the component - **Result:** Better separation since page-level headers are more specific **No nested layouts found** - all pages share the single root layout. --- ### 8. **Design Observations** - **Breadcrumb header is minimal**: 12px height, just text - **Page-level headers are rich**: Include descriptions, action buttons, filters -
